nodes.title: Trees and Recursion Refresher
objectives: Recall fundamental tree terminology (root, node, child, parent, leaf, height, depth).
Understand basic tree representations (e.g., linked list of nodes).
Review the concept of recursion and its application in tree algorithms.

nodes.title: Introduction to Binary Search Trees
objectives: Define a Binary Search Tree (BST) and its core properties.
Differentiate BSTs from general binary trees.
Identify valid and invalid BST structures.

nodes.title: BST Search Operation
objectives: Explain the algorithm for searching a value in a BST.
Trace the search operation on example BSTs.
Implement the search operation iteratively and recursively.

nodes.title: BST Insertion Operation
objectives: Describe the algorithm for inserting a new node into a BST.
Illustrate the insertion process with various examples.
Implement the insertion operation.

nodes.title: BST Traversal Methods
objectives: Explain In-order, Pre-order, and Post-order traversal algorithms for BSTs.
Perform each traversal on given BST examples.
Understand the significance of In-order traversal for BSTs (sorted output).

nodes.title: Finding Min/Max, Successor, and Predecessor in BST
objectives: Develop algorithms to find the minimum and maximum values in a BST.
Define and implement algorithms for finding the in-order successor of a node.
Define and implement algorithms for finding the in-order predecessor of a node.

nodes.title: BST Deletion Operation (Part 1: 0 or 1 Child)
objectives: Understand the complexities of node deletion in a BST.
Implement deletion for nodes with no children (leaf nodes).
Implement deletion for nodes with one child.

nodes.title: BST Deletion Operation (Part 2: 2 Children)
objectives: Explain the strategy for deleting a node with two children (using in-order successor or predecessor).
Implement the deletion algorithm for nodes with two children.
Trace complete deletion scenarios on various BST structures.

nodes.title: Time and Space Complexity Analysis of BST Operations
objectives: Analyze the best, average, and worst-case time complexities for search, insertion, and deletion.
Understand the factors influencing BST performance (e.g., tree height).
Determine the space complexity requirements for BSTs and their operations.

nodes.title: Validating a Binary Search Tree
objectives: Identify common pitfalls that can lead to an invalid BST structure.
Develop an algorithm to check if a given binary tree satisfies the BST properties.
Implement a robust BST validation function.

nodes.title: Building BSTs from Data Structures
objectives: Construct a BST from a list or array of unsorted elements.
Understand how to build a balanced BST from a sorted array/list (introduction to balanced BST concept).
Implement functions to build BSTs from various input types.

nodes.title: Disadvantages and Skewed Trees
objectives: Recognize scenarios where BSTs degrade into linked lists (skewed trees).
Understand the performance implications of skewed BSTs (worst-case complexity).
Discuss the limitations of basic BSTs for certain data patterns.

nodes.title: Introduction to Self-Balancing BSTs
objectives: Explain the need for self-balancing BSTs.
Briefly introduce common self-balancing BSTs like AVL trees and Red-Black trees.
Understand the high-level concept of rotations and rebalancing.

nodes.title: Hands-on: Implementing a BST Class
objectives: Write a complete BST class with Node structure and methods for insertion, search, deletion, and traversals.
Test the implementation with various test cases, including edge cases.
Refine code for clarity and efficiency.

nodes.title: Common BST Problems and Applications
objectives: Solve common algorithmic problems using BST properties (e.g., Kth smallest element, range sum).
Identify real-world applications where BSTs are beneficial.
Analyze problem constraints to determine if a BST is an appropriate data structure choice.

nodes.title: Introduction to REST APIs and Why Design Matters
objectives: Recall the fundamental concepts of REST and its architectural style.
Understand the importance of good API design for usability, maintainability, and scalability.
Differentiate between well-designed and poorly-designed APIs.

nodes.title: Understanding REST Architectural Constraints
objectives: Explain the six core architectural constraints of REST (Client-Server, Stateless, Cacheable, Uniform Interface, Layered System, Code-On-Demand).
Analyze how each constraint contributes to the benefits of RESTful systems.
Identify violations of REST principles in common API patterns.

nodes.title: Resource Modeling and URI Design Best Practices
objectives: Identify and model resources effectively using nouns.
Design clear, predictable, and hierarchical URIs.
Apply best practices for URI naming, including pluralization and nesting.

nodes.title: HTTP Methods: Mapping to CRUD and Idempotency
objectives: Map standard HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) to CRUD operations.
Understand the concepts of safety and idempotency in the context of HTTP methods.
Choose the appropriate HTTP method for various API operations.

nodes.title: Comprehensive Guide to HTTP Status Codes
objectives: Utilize 2xx, 3xx, 4xx, and 5xx status codes correctly for API responses.
Differentiate between common status codes like 200, 201, 204, 400, 401, 403, 404, 409, 500.
Ensure API responses convey clear semantic meaning through status codes.

nodes.title: Request and Response Formats: JSON and Content Negotiation
objectives: Understand the prevalence and benefits of JSON for API data exchange.
Discuss alternatives like XML and their use cases.
Implement content negotiation using HTTP headers (Accept, Content-Type).

nodes.title: API Versioning: Strategies and Considerations
objectives: Evaluate different API versioning strategies (URI, Header, Media Type).
Choose an appropriate versioning strategy based on project requirements and maintainability.
Understand the trade-offs and implications of each versioning approach.

nodes.title: Designing for Data Retrieval: Pagination, Filtering, and Sorting
objectives: Implement effective pagination strategies (offset-based, cursor-based).
Design flexible filtering mechanisms using query parameters.
Enable sorting capabilities for resource collections.
Address performance considerations for large datasets.

nodes.title: Consistent Error Handling and Custom Error Objects
objectives: Design a consistent and informative error response structure for your API.
Map specific error conditions to appropriate HTTP status codes.
Implement custom error objects to provide detailed error messages and codes.
Handle validation errors gracefully.

nodes.title: Authentication and Authorization in REST APIs
objectives: Understand common authentication methods for APIs (API Keys, JWT, OAuth2).
Implement authorization checks based on user roles and permissions.
Discuss best practices for securing API endpoints.
Distinguish between authentication and authorization.

nodes.title: HATEOAS and Hypermedia for Discoverability
objectives: Explain the principle of HATEOAS (Hypermedia as the Engine of Application State).
Implement hypermedia controls (links) in API responses.
Understand how HATEOAS improves API discoverability and evolvability.
Evaluate scenarios where HATEOAS is most beneficial.

nodes.title: Advanced API Security Considerations
objectives: Identify and mitigate common API security vulnerabilities (e.g., injection, mass assignment).
Implement input validation and output encoding best practices.
Understand the role of CORS, rate limiting, and throttling in API security.
Discuss data encryption and secure communication (HTTPS).

nodes.title: Documenting Your API with OpenAPI/Swagger
objectives: Understand the importance of comprehensive API documentation.
Learn to use OpenAPI Specification (Swagger) to describe REST APIs.
Generate interactive API documentation and client SDKs.
Maintain documentation alongside API development.

nodes.title: Designing for API Evolutability and Deprecation
objectives: Plan for future changes and extensions to your API.
Implement strategies for backward compatibility and graceful deprecation.
Communicate API changes effectively to consumers.
Understand the long-term maintenance aspects of API design.

nodes.title: Practical REST API Design Case Study
objectives: Apply all learned best practices to design a sample REST API for a given problem domain.
Justify design decisions based on REST principles and best practices.
Identify potential issues and propose solutions for a real-world API scenario.
Critique and refine an existing API design.

nodes.title: Introduction to Docker and Containers
objectives: Define what Docker is and its core purpose.
Explain the concept of containerization.
Identify the benefits of using containers in software development and deployment.

nodes.title: Docker Containers vs. Virtual Machines: Understanding the Differences
objectives: Compare and contrast Docker containers with traditional Virtual Machines (VMs).
Understand the architectural differences between containers and VMs.
Recognize scenarios where containers are preferred over VMs and vice-versa.

nodes.title: Installing Docker Desktop on Your System
objectives: Successfully download and install Docker Desktop on Windows, macOS, or Linux.
Verify the Docker installation and check its version.
Understand the components installed with Docker Desktop (Docker Engine, CLI, Compose).

nodes.title: Running Your First Docker Container
objectives: Execute the 'docker run' command to start a container.
Understand the basic syntax and common flags for 'docker run' (e.g., -it, --rm).
Run a simple 'hello-world' and an interactive container.

nodes.title: Managing Docker Containers: Lifecycle Commands
objectives: List running and stopped containers using 'docker ps'.
Start, stop, restart, and remove containers using 'docker start', 'stop', 'restart', 'rm'.
Inspect container details with 'docker inspect' and view logs with 'docker logs'.

nodes.title: Understanding Docker Images: The Blueprint for Containers
objectives: Define what a Docker Image is and its relationship to containers.
Explain the concept of image layers and how they contribute to efficiency.
Differentiate between an image and a container.

nodes.title: Pulling and Searching Docker Images from Registries
objectives: Search for public images on Docker Hub using 'docker search'.
Download images from Docker Hub using 'docker pull'.
List local images with 'docker images' and remove them with 'docker rmi'.

nodes.title: Creating Custom Images with Dockerfiles (Part 1: Basics)
objectives: Understand the purpose and basic syntax of a Dockerfile.
Learn essential Dockerfile instructions: FROM, RUN, CMD.
Create a simple Dockerfile to package a basic application.

nodes.title: Creating Custom Images with Dockerfiles (Part 2: Advanced)
objectives: Utilize additional Dockerfile instructions: COPY, ADD, EXPOSE, WORKDIR, ENTRYPOINT.
Understand the difference between CMD and ENTRYPOINT.
Apply best practices for writing efficient and secure Dockerfiles (e.g., multi-stage builds introduction).

nodes.title: Building and Tagging Docker Images
objectives: Build a Docker image from a Dockerfile using 'docker build'.
Understand and apply image tagging conventions for versioning and organization.
Rebuild and update an existing Docker image.

nodes.title: Pushing Images to Docker Hub
objectives: Create an account on Docker Hub.
Authenticate with Docker Hub from the command line using 'docker login'.
Push a custom-built Docker image to a public repository on Docker Hub.

nodes.title: Docker Volumes: Achieving Data Persistence
objectives: Explain why data persistence is crucial in containerized environments.
Differentiate between bind mounts and Docker volumes.
Implement bind mounts and named volumes to persist data for containers.

nodes.title: Docker Networking: Connecting Containers
objectives: Understand the default bridge network in Docker.
Publish container ports to the host machine.
Connect containers within the same network.

nodes.title: Introduction to Docker Compose: Orchestrating Multi-Container Apps
objectives: Explain the need for Docker Compose in multi-service applications.
Describe the core components of Docker Compose (services, networks, volumes).
Understand the benefits of using Compose for development environments.

nodes.title: Writing Your First Docker Compose File
objectives: Structure a basic 'docker-compose.yml' file.
Define multiple services, specify their images, ports, and volumes.
Create a simple multi-container application using Compose (e.g., web app with database).

nodes.title: Managing Docker Compose Applications
objectives: Start and stop multi-container applications using 'docker compose up' and 'down'.
View the status and logs of Compose services.
Execute commands within services defined in a Compose file.

nodes.title: Docker Best Practices and Basic Troubleshooting
objectives: Identify common Docker best practices for images and containers.
Learn basic troubleshooting techniques for Docker containers (e.g., checking logs, inspecting).
Understand how to clean up unused Docker resources.

nodes.title: Review of Docker Fundamentals and Next Steps
objectives: Consolidate understanding of core Docker concepts and commands.
Identify resources for continued learning in Docker and containerization.
Reflect on potential real-world applications of Docker skills.

nodes.title: Functions and Graphs Review
objectives: Recall the definition of a function, domain, and range.
Practice graphing common functions (linear, quadratic, polynomial, rational, basic trigonometric).
Understand function notation and evaluation.

nodes.title: Lines, Slopes, and Rates of Change
objectives: Calculate the slope of a line given two points or an equation.
Write equations of lines in various forms (slope-intercept, point-slope).
Understand the concept of average rate of change from precalculus.

nodes.title: Introduction to Limits: Intuitive Approach
objectives: Understand the intuitive meaning of a limit.
Estimate limits graphically and numerically.
Identify when a limit does not exist.

nodes.title: Properties of Limits and Direct Substitution
objectives: Apply basic limit properties (sum, difference, product, quotient, constant multiple).
Use direct substitution to evaluate limits of polynomial and rational functions.
Handle indeterminate forms by algebraic manipulation (factoring, rationalizing).

nodes.title: Limits at Infinity and Asymptotes
objectives: Evaluate limits as x approaches infinity or negative infinity.
Identify horizontal asymptotes of rational functions.
Understand vertical asymptotes in relation to infinite limits.

nodes.title: Continuity of Functions
objectives: Define continuity at a point and on an interval.
Identify types of discontinuities (removable, jump, infinite).
Apply the Intermediate Value Theorem (IVT).

nodes.title: Secant Lines, Tangent Lines, and Instantaneous Rate of Change
objectives: Distinguish between secant lines and tangent lines.
Understand the geometric interpretation of the slope of a tangent line.
Relate the slope of the tangent line to instantaneous rate of change.

nodes.title: Definition of the Derivative (Limit Definition)
objectives: State and apply the limit definition of the derivative.
Calculate derivatives of simple functions using the limit definition.
Understand the notation for derivatives (f'(x), dy/dx).

nodes.title: Basic Differentiation Rules
objectives: Apply the constant rule, power rule, constant multiple rule, sum rule, and difference rule.
Differentiate polynomial functions efficiently.
Find the equation of a tangent line to a curve at a given point.

nodes.title: Product and Quotient Rules
objectives: Apply the product rule to differentiate functions that are products of two expressions.
Apply the quotient rule to differentiate functions that are quotients of two expressions.
Solve problems involving rates of change using these rules.

nodes.title: The Chain Rule
objectives: Understand the concept of composite functions.
Apply the chain rule to differentiate composite functions.
Solve complex differentiation problems by combining the chain rule with other rules.

nodes.title: Derivatives of Trigonometric Functions
objectives: Memorize and apply the derivatives of sine, cosine, tangent, cotangent, secant, and cosecant functions.
Differentiate trigonometric functions combined with other rules (product, quotient, chain rule).

nodes.title: Derivatives of Exponential and Logarithmic Functions
objectives: Apply the differentiation rules for e^x and ln(x).
Differentiate exponential and logarithmic functions using the chain rule.
Understand logarithmic differentiation for complex functions.

nodes.title: Implicit Differentiation
objectives: Understand when and why implicit differentiation is necessary.
Apply implicit differentiation to find dy/dx for equations not explicitly solved for y.
Find the slope of a tangent line to implicitly defined curves.

nodes.title: Related Rates Problems
objectives: Identify and set up related rates problems.
Use implicit differentiation to solve problems involving rates of change of related quantities.
Solve common related rates scenarios (e.g., ladder problems, volume changes).

nodes.title: Higher Order Derivatives
objectives: Calculate second, third, and higher order derivatives.
Understand the physical interpretation of the second derivative (acceleration).
Apply higher order derivatives in context.

nodes.title: Extrema, Critical Points, and the First Derivative Test
objectives: Define absolute and local extrema.
Identify critical points of a function.
Apply the First Derivative Test to find intervals of increasing/decreasing and local extrema.

nodes.title: Concavity, Inflection Points, and the Second Derivative Test
objectives: Determine intervals of concavity (concave up/down).
Identify inflection points.
Apply the Second Derivative Test to classify local extrema.

nodes.title: Optimization Problems
objectives: Set up and solve real-world optimization problems.
Apply the First and Second Derivative Tests to find absolute maximums and minimums.
Solve problems involving maximizing area, volume, or minimizing cost.

nodes.title: Curve Sketching
objectives: Integrate knowledge of limits, asymptotes, intercepts, and derivatives to sketch graphs of functions.
Analyze a function's behavior (increasing/decreasing, concavity) to accurately draw its graph.

nodes.title: Antiderivatives and Indefinite Integrals
objectives: Understand the concept of an antiderivative as the reverse of differentiation.
Find the general antiderivative of basic functions.
Introduce the notation for indefinite integrals and the constant of integration.

nodes.title: Basic Integration Rules
objectives: Apply the power rule for integration.
Integrate constant multiples, sums, and differences of functions.
Integrate basic trigonometric functions (sine, cosine, secant squared).

nodes.title: Area Under a Curve: Riemann Sums
objectives: Approximate the area under a curve using left, right, and midpoint Riemann sums.
Understand the concept of a limit of Riemann sums leading to the definite integral.
Interpret area as a sum of infinitesimally thin rectangles.

nodes.title: The Definite Integral and Its Properties
objectives: Define the definite integral as the limit of Riemann sums.
Understand the geometric interpretation of the definite integral as net signed area.
Apply properties of definite integrals (e.g., constant multiple, sum/difference, interval additivity).

nodes.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part 1
objectives: Understand the relationship between differentiation and integration.
Apply FTC Part 1 to find the derivative of an integral with a variable upper limit.
Recognize the significance of FTC in connecting the two main branches of calculus.

nodes.title: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus Part 2 (Evaluation Theorem)
objectives: Apply FTC Part 2 to evaluate definite integrals using antiderivatives.
Calculate exact areas under curves.
Solve problems involving total change and net change.

nodes.title: Integration by U-Substitution
objectives: Recognize when u-substitution is an appropriate integration technique.
Apply u-substitution to evaluate indefinite and definite integrals.
Handle changes of limits for definite integrals with u-substitution.

nodes.title: Applications of Integrals: Area Between Curves & Average Value
objectives: Calculate the area between two curves.
Find the average value of a function over a given interval.
Solve basic application problems using definite integrals (e.g., displacement, total change).

nodes.title: Introduction to Vectors and Matrices
objectives: Define vectors and matrices and understand their basic properties.
Perform vector addition, scalar multiplication, and dot products.
Execute matrix addition, scalar multiplication, and matrix multiplication.
Identify special types of matrices (e.g., identity, zero, diagonal).

nodes.title: Solving Systems of Linear Equations
objectives: Represent systems of linear equations in matrix form (Ax=b).
Solve systems using Gaussian elimination and Gauss-Jordan elimination.
Understand the concepts of row echelon form and reduced row echelon form.
Determine the consistency and number of solutions for a system.

nodes.title: Matrix Inverses and Determinants
objectives: Compute the inverse of a square matrix using row operations and the adjugate method.
Understand the properties and applications of matrix inverses.
Calculate determinants for 2x2 and 3x3 matrices using cofactor expansion.
Apply properties of determinants to simplify calculations and determine invertibility.

nodes.title: Vector Spaces and Subspaces
objectives: Define an abstract vector space and its axioms.
Identify common vector spaces (e.g., R^n, P_n, M_mn).
Define a subspace and verify if a given subset is a subspace.
Understand the concepts of linear combination, span, and linear independence/dependence.

nodes.title: Basis and Dimension
objectives: Define a basis for a vector space and find a basis for common vector spaces.
Understand the concept of dimension and calculate the dimension of a vector space/subspace.
Explain coordinate vectors and how to find them relative to a given basis.
Perform a change of basis between different bases.

nodes.title: Linear Transformations
objectives: Define a linear transformation and identify its properties.
Find the matrix representation of a linear transformation.
Understand the concepts of one-to-one (injective) and onto (surjective) transformations.
Relate linear transformations to geometric operations (e.g., rotation, scaling, projection).

nodes.title: Kernel, Range, and Rank-Nullity Theorem
objectives: Define the kernel (null space) and range (column space) of a linear transformation/matrix.
Compute bases for the kernel and range of a given matrix.
State and apply the Rank-Nullity Theorem.
Understand the relationship between the dimensions of the kernel and range.

nodes.title: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
objectives: Define eigenvalues and eigenvectors and understand their significance.
Compute eigenvalues by solving the characteristic equation.
Find eigenvectors corresponding to specific eigenvalues.
Determine if a matrix is diagonalizable and perform matrix diagonalization.

nodes.title: Inner Product Spaces and Orthogonality
objectives: Define an inner product and understand its properties.
Calculate dot products and general inner products in various vector spaces.
Understand concepts of orthogonality, orthonormal bases, and orthogonal complements.
Apply the Gram-Schmidt orthogonalization process to construct orthonormal bases.

nodes.title: Symmetric Matrices and Orthogonal Diagonalization
objectives: Identify properties of symmetric matrices.
Understand the Spectral Theorem for symmetric matrices.
Perform orthogonal diagonalization of symmetric matrices.
Apply orthogonal diagonalization in contexts like quadratic forms.

nodes.title: Practical Applications of Linear Algebra
objectives: Explore real-world applications of linear algebra (e.g., least squares, network analysis, image processing).
Understand the basic principles behind Principal Component Analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction.
Discuss the role of eigenvalues and eigenvectors in practical scenarios like stability analysis or PageRank.
Interpret the results of linear algebra methods in applied contexts.

nodes.title: Introduction to Singular Value Decomposition (SVD)
objectives: Understand the conceptual basis and purpose of Singular Value Decomposition (SVD).
Interpret the components of SVD: singular values, left and right singular vectors.
Recognize the broad applications of SVD in data compression, noise reduction, and recommender systems.
Briefly relate SVD to other matrix decompositions and its geometric interpretation.

nodes.title: Introduction to Classical Mechanics and Physics Fundamentals
objectives: Define classical mechanics and its scope.
Understand the importance of studying classical mechanics.
Identify fundamental physical quantities like length, mass, and time.
Recognize common units in the SI system.

nodes.title: Essential Mathematical Tools: Scalars, Vectors, and Basic Trigonometry
objectives: Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities.
Perform basic vector operations (addition, subtraction) graphically and analytically.
Resolve vectors into their rectangular components.
Apply basic trigonometric functions (sine, cosine, tangent) to vector problems.

nodes.title: Describing Motion: Kinematics in One Dimension
objectives: Define position, displacement, distance, speed, velocity, and acceleration.
Distinguish between average and instantaneous velocity/acceleration.
Solve problems involving constant acceleration in one dimension using kinematic equations.
Interpret position-time, velocity-time, and acceleration-time graphs.

nodes.title: Extending Motion: Basic Kinematics in Two Dimensions
objectives: Represent 2D motion using vector components.
Apply kinematic equations independently to the x and y components of motion.
Understand the independence of horizontal and vertical motion.
Solve basic projectile motion problems (without air resistance).

nodes.title: Understanding Force: The Cause of Motion Changes
objectives: Define force as a push or a pull.
Identify different types of forces (contact vs. field forces).
Understand the SI unit of force (Newton).
Explain the concept of net force.

nodes.title: Newton's First Law: The Law of Inertia and Equilibrium
objectives: State Newton's First Law of Motion.
Define inertia and relate it to mass.
Identify conditions for an object to be in equilibrium (at rest or constant velocity).
Differentiate between balanced and unbalanced forces.

nodes.title: Mass, Weight, and Gravitational Force
objectives: Differentiate clearly between mass and weight.
Understand mass as a measure of inertia.
Calculate the weight of an object using the acceleration due to gravity.
Explain how weight can change while mass remains constant.

nodes.title: Newton's Second Law: Force, Mass, and Acceleration (F=ma)
objectives: State Newton's Second Law of Motion.
Relate net force, mass, and acceleration using the equation F=ma.
Solve simple problems involving F=ma in one dimension.
Understand the vector nature of F=ma.

nodes.title: Visualizing Forces: Introduction to Free-Body Diagrams
objectives: Learn the steps to draw an accurate free-body diagram for an object.
Identify all forces acting on an object in a given scenario.
Choose an appropriate coordinate system for problem-solving.
Use free-body diagrams as a tool to apply Newton's Laws.

nodes.title: Common Forces in Mechanics: Gravitational and Normal Force
objectives: Define and calculate the force of gravity (weight) acting on an object.
Understand the concept and direction of the normal force.
Determine the magnitude of the normal force in various scenarios (flat surfaces, inclined planes).
Apply these forces in free-body diagrams.

nodes.title: Common Forces in Mechanics: Friction and Tension
objectives: Define and differentiate between static and kinetic friction.
Calculate the force of static and kinetic friction using coefficients of friction.
Understand the concept of tension in ropes, strings, and cables.
Apply friction and tension forces in free-body diagrams and F=ma equations.

nodes.title: Newton's Third Law: Action-Reaction Pairs
objectives: State Newton's Third Law of Motion.
Identify action-reaction force pairs for interacting objects.
Differentiate between action-reaction pairs and balanced forces.
Explain why action-reaction pairs do not cancel each other out.

nodes.title: Applying Newton's Laws: Single Object Problems
objectives: Develop a systematic approach to solving problems using Newton's Laws.
Apply free-body diagrams and Newton's Second Law to solve problems involving a single object in 1D.
Solve problems involving a single object on horizontal and vertical surfaces.
Calculate acceleration and unknown forces in various scenarios.

nodes.title: Applying Newton's Laws: Multi-Object and Inclined Plane Problems
objectives: Solve problems involving multiple connected objects (e.g., Atwood machines, blocks on tables).
Analyze motion on inclined planes using Newton's Laws and resolved force components.
Apply Newton's Laws to systems with friction and tension.
Practice setting up and solving systems of equations for complex scenarios.

nodes.title: Newton's Laws and Uniform Circular Motion
objectives: Define centripetal acceleration and centripetal force.
Relate the net force required for uniform circular motion to mass, velocity, and radius.
Solve problems involving objects moving in horizontal and vertical circles.
Apply Newton's Second Law to analyze forces in circular motion.

nodes.title: Review and Synthesis of Newton's Laws
objectives: Consolidate understanding of all three Newton's Laws and their interrelationships.
Practice a variety of problem types covering all concepts learned.
Identify common misconceptions related to Newton's Laws.
Develop strategies for approaching and solving complex dynamics problems.

nodes.title: Review of Vector Calculus for EM
objectives: Recall basic vector operations (dot product, cross product).
Understand gradient, divergence, and curl operators.
Review line, surface, and volume integrals.
Identify the physical significance of these operators in scalar and vector fields.

nodes.title: Introduction to Electric Charge and Current
objectives: Define electric charge and its fundamental properties (quantization, conservation).
Differentiate between conductors and insulators.
Define electric current, current density, and drift velocity.
Explain Ohm's Law and its microscopic interpretation.

nodes.title: Coulomb's Law and the Electric Field
objectives: State Coulomb's Law for point charges.
Calculate the electric force between multiple point charges using superposition.
Define the electric field and calculate it for point charges and simple discrete charge distributions.
Visualize electric field lines and their properties.

nodes.title: Electric Fields from Continuous Charge Distributions
objectives: Set up integral expressions for the electric field due to continuous charge distributions (line, surface, volume).
Calculate the electric field for simple symmetric continuous distributions (e.g., charged rod, ring, disk).
Understand the concept of charge density (linear, surface, volume).

nodes.title: Gauss's Law and Electric Flux
objectives: Define electric flux through an arbitrary surface.
State Gauss's Law in integral form.
Understand the relationship between enclosed charge and electric flux.
Apply Gauss's Law to calculate electric fields for highly symmetric charge distributions.

nodes.title: Electric Potential Energy and Potential
objectives: Define electric potential energy for a system of point charges.
Relate electric potential energy to the work done by the electric field.
Define electric potential (voltage) and potential difference.
Calculate potential difference for various electric fields.

nodes.title: Calculating Electric Potential from Charge Distributions
objectives: Derive the electric potential from the electric field using integration.
Calculate electric potential for point charges and continuous charge distributions.
Understand the relationship between electric field and electric potential (E = -grad V).
Sketch equipotential lines and relate them to electric field lines.

nodes.title: Capacitance and Capacitors
objectives: Define capacitance and calculate it for parallel-plate, spherical, and cylindrical capacitors.
Analyze capacitors in series and parallel circuits.
Calculate the energy stored in a capacitor.
Understand the concept of electric energy density.

nodes.title: Dielectrics and Electric Polarization
objectives: Explain the effect of dielectric materials on capacitance.
Define dielectric constant and electric susceptibility.
Describe electric polarization in dielectric materials.
Understand the concept of bound charges and their role in modifying electric fields.

nodes.title: Introduction to Magnetism and Magnetic Fields
objectives: Differentiate between electric and magnetic forces and fields.
Describe sources of magnetic fields (permanent magnets, moving charges, currents).
Visualize magnetic field lines and their properties.
Understand the concept of magnetic flux.

nodes.title: The Lorentz Force on Moving Charges
objectives: State the Lorentz force law for a charged particle moving in electric and magnetic fields.
Calculate the magnetic force on a single moving charge.
Analyze the motion of charged particles in uniform magnetic fields (e.g., cyclotron motion).
Understand applications like velocity selectors and mass spectrometers.

nodes.title: Magnetic Force on Current-Carrying Wires
objectives: Derive the magnetic force on a current-carrying wire segment in a magnetic field.
Calculate the force on straight wires and current loops.
Explain the concept of magnetic torque on a current loop.
Understand the principle of electric motors.

nodes.title: Biot-Savart Law for Magnetic Fields
objectives: State the Biot-Savart Law for calculating magnetic fields from current elements.
Apply the Biot-Savart Law to calculate magnetic fields for simple current configurations (e.g., long straight wire, circular loop on axis).
Understand the vector nature of the Biot-Savart Law and its integral form.

nodes.title: Ampere's Law in Magnetostatics
objectives: Define Ampere's Law in integral form.
Apply Ampere's Law to calculate magnetic fields for highly symmetric current distributions (e.g., infinitely long wire, solenoid, toroid).
Compare and contrast Ampere's Law with Gauss's Law.

nodes.title: Magnetic Dipoles and Magnetization
objectives: Define magnetic dipole moment for a current loop.
Understand the torque and potential energy of a magnetic dipole in an external magnetic field.
Explain the concept of magnetization and magnetic susceptibility.
Differentiate between diamagnetic, paramagnetic, and ferromagnetic materials.

nodes.title: Faraday's Law of Electromagnetic Induction
objectives: State Faraday's Law of Induction and explain its physical meaning.
Calculate induced EMF due to changing magnetic flux.
Understand the concept of motional EMF.
Identify factors affecting the magnitude and direction of induced currents.

nodes.title: Lenz's Law and Applications of Induction
objectives: Apply Lenz's Law to determine the direction of induced current and EMF.
Solve problems involving motional EMF in various scenarios (e.g., moving rod in a field).
Describe practical applications of electromagnetic induction (e.g., generators, transformers).
Understand eddy currents and their effects.

nodes.title: Inductance and Inductors
objectives: Define self-inductance and mutual inductance.
Calculate the inductance of simple geometries (e.g., solenoid, toroid).
Analyze inductors in series and parallel circuits.
Understand the energy stored in an inductor and magnetic energy density.

nodes.title: Review of RL and RC Circuits (Time-Varying)
objectives: Analyze the transient behavior of RL circuits (charging and discharging).
Analyze the transient behavior of RC circuits (charging and discharging).
Understand time constants for RL and RC circuits.
Solve differential equations describing these circuits.

nodes.title: Displacement Current and Modified Ampere's Law
objectives: Explain the concept of displacement current introduced by Maxwell.
Derive and state the modified Ampere's Law (Ampere-Maxwell Law).
Understand the necessity of displacement current for consistency with charge conservation.
Calculate displacement current in simple cases (e.g., charging capacitor).

nodes.title: Maxwell's Equations in Integral Form
objectives: State all four Maxwell's Equations in their integral form.
Explain the physical meaning and significance of each equation.
Identify which previous laws are unified by Maxwell's Equations.
Understand how these equations describe the interrelationship between electric and magnetic fields.

nodes.title: Qualitative Introduction to Electromagnetic Waves
objectives: Explain how Maxwell's equations predict the existence of electromagnetic waves.
Describe the basic properties of electromagnetic waves (transverse, self-propagating).
Relate the speed of light to fundamental constants (permeability and permittivity of free space).
Visualize the oscillating electric and magnetic fields in an EM wave.

nodes.title: The Electromagnetic Spectrum and Applications
objectives: Identify different regions of the electromagnetic spectrum (radio waves, microwaves, IR, visible, UV, X-rays, gamma rays).
Describe the general characteristics and common applications of each region.
Understand the relationship between wavelength, frequency, and energy for EM waves.
Discuss the generation and detection of different types of EM waves.

nodes.title: Energy and Momentum in Electromagnetic Waves
objectives: Define the Poynting vector and explain its significance for energy flow in EM waves.
Calculate the intensity of an electromagnetic wave.
Understand the concept of radiation pressure exerted by EM waves.
Relate energy and momentum of EM waves to their properties.

nodes.title: Boundary Conditions for Electric and Magnetic Fields
objectives: Derive and apply boundary conditions for electric fields (tangential E, normal D) at interfaces between different media.
Derive and apply boundary conditions for magnetic fields (tangential H, normal B) at interfaces.
Solve problems involving EM fields at boundaries (e.g., conductor-dielectric interface).
Understand the physical implications of these boundary conditions.

nodes.title: Electromagnetism Basics: Comprehensive Problem Solving Workshop
objectives: Apply all fundamental principles and laws of electromagnetism to solve complex problems.
Integrate concepts from electrostatics, magnetostatics, and electrodynamics.
Develop strategies for approaching multi-concept EM problems.
Reinforce understanding through diverse problem types and applications.

nodes.title: Preview of Advanced Electromagnetism Topics
objectives: Briefly introduce advanced concepts like Maxwell's equations in differential form, wave guides, and plasma physics.
Discuss the limitations of the 'basics' covered and the need for more advanced treatments.
Identify areas for further study in electromagnetism.
Reflect on the historical development and impact of EM theory.

nodes.title: Introduction to Financial Statement Analysis (FSA) and Its Purpose
objectives: Define financial statement analysis and its importance for various stakeholders.
Identify the primary financial statements used in analysis.
Understand the overall framework and steps involved in performing FSA.

nodes.title: Deep Dive into the Balance Sheet: Structure and Insights
objectives: Analyze the components of assets, liabilities, and equity on the balance sheet.
Interpret the balance sheet to understand a company's financial position and capital structure.
Identify key accounts and their implications for financial health.

nodes.title: Deep Dive into the Income Statement: Performance and Profitability
objectives: Analyze the components of revenue, expenses, and different profit metrics on the income statement.
Interpret the income statement to understand a company's operational performance and profitability.
Distinguish between operating and non-operating items and their impact.

nodes.title: Deep Dive into the Cash Flow Statement: Cash Generation and Usage
objectives: Analyze cash flows from operating, investing, and financing activities.
Understand the difference between direct and indirect methods of presenting operating cash flows.
Interpret the cash flow statement to assess a company's liquidity and solvency.

nodes.title: Interrelationships Among the Financial Statements
objectives: Explain how the balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement are interconnected.
Trace the impact of transactions across all three financial statements.
Utilize the relationships to identify inconsistencies or potential issues in financial reporting.

nodes.title: Horizontal (Trend) Analysis: Identifying Performance Changes Over Time
objectives: Perform horizontal analysis on financial statements to identify trends.
Calculate period-over-period and multi-period percentage changes.
Interpret trends in key financial figures to understand performance evolution.

nodes.title: Vertical (Common-Size) Analysis: Benchmarking Relative Proportions
objectives: Perform vertical analysis by expressing financial statement items as percentages of a base figure.
Interpret common-size financial statements to compare a company's structure over time or against peers.
Identify changes in the relative importance of accounts.

nodes.title: Liquidity and Solvency Ratios: Short-term and Long-term Health
objectives: Calculate and interpret key liquidity ratios (current, quick, cash).
Calculate and interpret key solvency ratios (debt-to-equity, debt-to-assets, interest coverage).
Assess a company's ability to meet its short-term and long-term obligations.

nodes.title: Profitability and Efficiency Ratios: Performance and Asset Utilization
objectives: Calculate and interpret key profitability ratios (gross margin, operating margin, net margin, ROA, ROE).
Calculate and interpret key efficiency ratios (inventory turnover, receivables turnover, asset turnover).
Evaluate a company's ability to generate profits and utilize its assets effectively.

nodes.title: Market and Valuation Ratios: Investor Perspective
objectives: Calculate and interpret basic market ratios (P/E, P/B, EPS, dividend yield).
Understand the basic concept of enterprise value multiples.
Relate financial statement analysis to market perception and valuation.

nodes.title: DuPont Framework Analysis: Deconstructing Return on Equity
objectives: Apply the DuPont framework to decompose Return on Equity (ROE) into its core components.
Identify the drivers of ROE: profitability, asset efficiency, and financial leverage.
Use DuPont analysis to pinpoint strengths and weaknesses in a company's financial strategy.

nodes.title: Quality of Earnings Analysis: Assessing Reporting Reliability
objectives: Identify red flags and potential aggressive accounting practices that can distort reported earnings.
Distinguish between sustainable and unsustainable earnings.
Evaluate the reliability of a company's reported profits and cash flows.

nodes.title: Free Cash Flow Analysis: Measuring Discretionary Cash
objectives: Calculate different measures of free cash flow (e.g., Free Cash Flow to Firm, Free Cash Flow to Equity).
Interpret free cash flow to assess a company's ability to fund growth, pay down debt, or distribute to shareholders.
Understand the importance of free cash flow for valuation and capital allocation decisions.

nodes.title: Segment and Geographical Analysis: Disaggregated Performance
objectives: Analyze financial performance at the business segment and geographical region level.
Identify high-performing and underperforming parts of a diversified company.
Understand the strategic implications of disaggregated data for investment decisions.

nodes.title: Limitations of FSA and Incorporating Qualitative Factors
objectives: Recognize the inherent limitations of financial statement analysis (e.g., historical data, accounting choices).
Identify key qualitative factors (management quality, corporate governance, brand strength) that influence performance.
Integrate qualitative assessments with quantitative analysis for a holistic view.

nodes.title: Forecasting Financial Performance: Introduction to Modeling
objectives: Understand the basic principles and steps of financial forecasting.
Apply simple forecasting methods for revenue, expenses, and asset accounts.
Construct basic projected financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow Statement).

nodes.title: Industry and Economic Analysis: Setting the Context for FSA
objectives: Understand how industry structure, competitive landscape, and macroeconomic factors impact company performance.
Integrate industry-specific trends and economic outlook into financial analysis.
Benchmark a company's performance against industry averages and competitors.

nodes.title: Integrated Financial Analysis: Comprehensive Case Study
objectives: Apply all learned financial statement analysis techniques to a real-world company case.
Develop a comprehensive financial analysis report, including quantitative and qualitative insights.
Formulate well-reasoned conclusions and recommendations based on the analysis.

nodes.title: What is a Project? What is Project Management?
objectives: Define what constitutes a 'project' and differentiate it from routine operations.
Understand the purpose and value of project management.
Identify key characteristics of a successful project.

nodes.title: The Role and Responsibilities of a Project Manager
objectives: Describe the primary duties and responsibilities of a Project Manager.
Identify essential skills for effective project management.
Understand the importance of leadership and communication in project success.

nodes.title: Understanding the Project Life Cycle
objectives: Explain the typical phases of a project life cycle.
Recognize how project complexity influences the life cycle.
Understand the concept of project deliverables at each phase.

nodes.title: Initiating a Project: Defining the Vision
objectives: Describe the activities involved in the project initiating phase.
Understand the purpose of a Project Charter.
Identify key stakeholders and their initial involvement.

nodes.title: Planning Fundamentals: Laying the Groundwork
objectives: Explain the importance of comprehensive project planning.
Identify the key outputs of the planning process group.
Understand the iterative nature of planning.

nodes.title: Scope Management: What Are We Building?
objectives: Define project scope and its importance.
Explain how to create a Work Breakdown Structure (WBS).
Understand the concept of scope creep and how to manage it.

nodes.title: Schedule Management: When Will It Be Done?
objectives: Understand techniques for estimating activity durations.
Explain how to develop a project schedule.
Introduce Gantt charts and critical path method concepts.

nodes.title: Cost Management: How Much Will It Cost?
objectives: Understand the principles of project cost estimation.
Learn how to create a project budget.
Identify common cost control techniques.

nodes.title: Risk Management: What Could Go Wrong?
objectives: Define project risk and its impact.
Understand the process of identifying and analyzing project risks.
Learn basic strategies for planning risk responses.

nodes.title: Executing the Project: Doing the Work
objectives: Describe the activities involved in the project executing phase.
Understand the importance of team management and leadership during execution.
Explain how to manage project resources effectively.

nodes.title: Monitoring & Controlling: Keeping the Project on Track
objectives: Understand the purpose of monitoring and controlling project progress.
Identify methods for tracking schedule and cost performance.
Explain how to manage changes to the project plan.

nodes.title: Communication and Stakeholder Management
objectives: Understand the importance of effective project communication.
Identify different communication methods and channels.
Learn strategies for engaging and managing project stakeholders.

nodes.title: Closing the Project: Finalizing and Learning
objectives: Describe the activities involved in formally closing a project or phase.
Understand the importance of lessons learned documentation.
Explain how to gain final acceptance for project deliverables.

nodes.title: Introduction to Agile Project Management Concepts
objectives: Understand the core values and principles of the Agile Manifesto.
Differentiate between traditional (waterfall) and Agile approaches.
Identify common Agile frameworks like Scrum or Kanban (brief overview).

nodes.title: Practical Application: Managing a Simple Project Scenario
objectives: Apply fundamental project management concepts to a hypothetical scenario.
Practice identifying project phases and key deliverables.
Develop a basic project plan outline for a given problem.

nodes.title: Introduction to Biology and Life
objectives: Define biology as a scientific discipline.
List and describe the fundamental characteristics of living organisms.
Understand the hierarchical organization of life from atoms to the biosphere.

nodes.title: Basic Chemistry for Cell Biology
objectives: Recall fundamental concepts of atoms, elements, and chemical bonds.
Describe the properties of water and its importance for life.
Understand the basics of pH and buffers in biological systems.

nodes.title: The Four Macromolecules of Life
objectives: Identify the four major classes of biological macromolecules: carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids.
Describe the basic building blocks (monomers) and polymers for each class.
Explain the primary functions of each macromolecule within a cell.

nodes.title: Exploring Cells: Microscopy Basics
objectives: Explain the basic principles of light microscopy.
Differentiate between resolution and magnification.
Identify the major components of a light microscope and their functions.

nodes.title: Prokaryotic vs. Eukaryotic Cells
objectives: Distinguish between prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells based on their structural features.
Provide examples of organisms that are prokaryotic and eukaryotic.
Understand the evolutionary significance of these two cell types.

nodes.title: Overview of Eukaryotic Cell Structure
objectives: Identify the major organelles found in a typical animal and plant eukaryotic cell.
Describe the general function of each major organelle.
Understand the concept of cellular compartmentalization.

nodes.title: The Cell Membrane: Structure and Function
objectives: Describe the fluid mosaic model of the cell membrane.
Identify the main components of the cell membrane (phospholipids, proteins, carbohydrates, cholesterol).
Explain the role of each component in membrane structure and function.

nodes.title: Membrane Transport: Moving Molecules Across the Cell
objectives: Differentiate between passive transport (diffusion, facilitated diffusion, osmosis) and active transport.
Explain how different types of molecules cross the cell membrane.
Understand the role of transport proteins and energy in active transport.

nodes.title: The Nucleus: Control Center of the Cell
objectives: Describe the structure and function of the cell nucleus.
Explain how DNA is organized into chromatin and chromosomes.
Understand the role of the nuclear envelope and nuclear pores.

nodes.title: ER and Golgi: Protein Synthesis and Processing
objectives: Describe the structure and interconnected functions of the endoplasmic reticulum (rough and smooth).
Explain the role of the Golgi apparatus in modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids.
Understand the secretory pathway within the cell.

nodes.title: Mitochondria: The Powerhouses of the Cell
objectives: Describe the structure of mitochondria.
Explain the basic process of cellular respiration and its purpose.
Understand how mitochondria generate ATP to power cellular activities.

nodes.title: Chloroplasts: Capturing Light Energy (Plant Cells)
objectives: Identify chloroplasts as key organelles in plant and algal cells.
Describe the basic structure of a chloroplast.
Understand the overall process of photosynthesis and its importance for life on Earth.

nodes.title: Lysosomes, Peroxisomes, and Vacuoles: Waste Management and Storage
objectives: Explain the function of lysosomes in cellular digestion and waste breakdown.
Describe the role of peroxisomes in detoxification.
Understand the diverse functions of vacuoles in plant and animal cells.

nodes.title: The Cytoskeleton: Support and Movement
objectives: Identify the three main types of cytoskeletal filaments: microtubules, microfilaments, and intermediate filaments.
Describe the primary functions of the cytoskeleton in maintaining cell shape, enabling movement, and intracellular transport.
Understand how motor proteins interact with the cytoskeleton.

nodes.title: Cell Junctions and Adhesion: Connecting Cells
objectives: Describe the different types of cell junctions (tight junctions, desmosomes, gap junctions) found in animal cells.
Explain the function of each type of cell junction.
Understand the role of cell adhesion molecules in tissue formation.

nodes.title: The Cell Cycle: Life and Division of a Cell
objectives: Outline the main phases of the eukaryotic cell cycle (Interphase and M phase).
Describe the key events occurring in G1, S, G2, and M phases.
Understand the concept of cell cycle checkpoints.

nodes.title: Mitosis: Somatic Cell Division
objectives: Describe the stages of mitosis (prophase, metaphase, anaphase, telophase).
Explain the process of cytokinesis.
Understand the outcome of mitosis: two genetically identical daughter cells.

nodes.title: Meiosis: Forming Gametes for Reproduction
objectives: Describe the stages of meiosis I and meiosis II.
Explain the significance of crossing over and independent assortment in generating genetic variation.
Compare and contrast mitosis and meiosis, highlighting their different outcomes.

nodes.title: Introduction to Genetics: Mendelian Principles
objectives: Explain Mendel's laws of inheritance (segregation and independent assortment).
Define key genetic terms: gene, allele, genotype, phenotype, homozygous, heterozygous.
Solve basic monohybrid and dihybrid cross problems using Punnett squares.

nodes.title: DNA: The Molecule of Heredity
objectives: Describe the double helix structure of DNA.
Identify the components of a DNA nucleotide (sugar, phosphate, nitrogenous base).
Explain how complementary base pairing (A-T, G-C) is crucial for DNA structure and function.

nodes.title: DNA Replication: Copying the Genetic Material
objectives: Explain the semi-conservative model of DNA replication.
Identify the key enzymes involved in DNA replication (e.g., DNA polymerase, helicase).
Outline the basic steps of DNA replication.

nodes.title: RNA and Transcription: From DNA to RNA
objectives: Compare and contrast the structure and types of RNA (mRNA, tRNA, rRNA) with DNA.
Describe the process of transcription, including initiation, elongation, and termination.
Understand the role of RNA polymerase in synthesizing RNA from a DNA template.

nodes.title: Protein Synthesis: Translation and the Genetic Code
objectives: Explain how the genetic code translates mRNA sequences into amino acid sequences.
Describe the process of translation, including initiation, elongation, and termination.
Identify the roles of ribosomes, mRNA, and tRNA in protein synthesis.

nodes.title: Basic Concepts of Gene Expression Regulation
objectives: Understand why gene expression needs to be regulated.
Describe simple mechanisms of gene regulation in prokaryotes (e.g., operons).
Briefly explain that gene regulation in eukaryotes is more complex.

nodes.title: Mutations: Changes in Genetic Information
objectives: Define what a gene mutation is and its potential causes.
Differentiate between point mutations and chromosomal mutations.
Explain how mutations can lead to altered proteins and genetic diseases.

nodes.title: Introduction to Biotechnology and Genetic Engineering
objectives: Define biotechnology and genetic engineering.
Describe basic concepts of common biotechnology techniques (e.g., PCR, gene cloning).
Understand the potential applications of genetic engineering in medicine and agriculture.

nodes.title: Ethical Considerations in Genetics and Biotechnology
objectives: Identify ethical issues associated with genetic testing and genetic engineering.
Discuss the societal implications of advancements in cell biology and genetics.
Encourage critical thinking about the responsible use of genetic technologies.
